title: excoecaria laurocerasus var. laurifolia
intro: Excoecaria laurocerasus var. laurifolia, also known as the poison tree or Australian poison tree, is a plant species native to tropical Australia, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant contains toxic compounds, including phorbol esters, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Excoecaria laurocerasus var. laurifolia is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
